task_completion.description: The experiment is created and saved as spotlight_search.psyexp in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder window title shows 'spotlight_search.psyexp', or a file explorer/save dialog confirms the file is saved in '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/'.

task_completion.description: A loop is created in the experiment flow that references a conditions CSV file for the stimuli.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Loop Properties dialog in PsychoPy Builder shows a CSV file loaded in the 'Conditions' field, and the summary text below it should indicate it successfully loaded conditions (representing the 3 images).

task_completion.description: An Image component is added to the routine to display the search arrays.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Image component icon is visible in the routine timeline, and its properties show the 'Image' field set to a variable from the conditions file (e.g., $image_file) set to update 'every repeat'.

task_completion.description: A Mouse component is added to the routine to track gaze/cursor position.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Mouse component icon is visible in the routine timeline.

task_completion.description: An Aperture component is added to the routine to restrict the visible area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Aperture component icon is visible in the routine timeline.

task_completion.description: The Aperture component is configured to be the correct size and track the mouse continuously.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Aperture component properties, the 'Size' field is set to 150 (if units are pixels) or 0.2 (if units are height). The 'Position' field is set to the mouse position (e.g., `mouse.getPos()`) and the dropdown next to it is set to 'set every frame' to ensure continuous tracking.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding components, editing properties dialogs) rather than writing a raw .psyexp XML/JSON file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the Builder requirement by writing a pure Python script in PsychoPy Coder.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main workspace shown is the 'Builder' view with the Routine and Flow panels, not the 'Coder' view with a raw Python script.

integrity.description: The agent actually created or edited a CSV file for the conditions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a spreadsheet editor or text editor open with a CSV file containing paths or filenames for the 3 images in the stimuli directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/spotlight_search.psyexp'. The stimuli directory is '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/stimuli' and contains three images ('array_1.png', 'array_2.png', 'array_3.png') which should be referenced in the conditions CSV file.
